paper_title: Establishing a protocol for in vitro pollen germination

methods.method_name: In Vitro Pollen Germination Protocol Establishment
methods.description: This method describes the process of establishing a culture medium protocol for in vitro pollen germination using the D'Água cultivar. The protocol involves optimizing various parameters such as agar concentration, pH, sucrose concentration, calcium nitrate concentration, boric acid concentration, incubation temperature, and pollen tube emission time to achieve maximum germination.

steps.description: The anthers were removed from ten flower buds collected in the late afternoon using tweezers, and stored in open petri dishes at a controlled temperature (27°C) for 12 hours.
steps.searchable_summary: Collect and store anthers from flower buds at 27°C for 12 hours

steps.description: Conduct experiments to determine optimal concentrations of agar, pH, sucrose, calcium nitrate, boric acid, incubation temperature, and pollen tube emission time.
steps.searchable_summary: Conduct sequential experiments to optimize culture medium parameters

steps.description: Spread pollen along the surface of the petri dish containing 20 ml of culture medium for uniform distribution.
steps.searchable_summary: Distribute pollen uniformly on culture medium surface
outputs: uniformly distributed pollen on culture medium

steps.description: Four hours after inoculation, view the pollen grains under a monocular microscope with a 10x objective to quantify the germinated pollen grains.
steps.searchable_summary: Quantify germinated pollen grains using microscopy

steps.description: Analyze the effect of different parameters on pollen germination and determine the optimal conditions.
steps.searchable_summary: Analyze parameter effects and determine optimal germination conditions
